t54.subject: Fix broken test build: double-nested mod tests bug
t54.description: The previous session ended with a broken `cargo test` build. `input/tests.rs` and `render/tests.rs` both wrap content in `mod tests { use super::*; }` but the parent `input.rs`/`render.rs` already declares `mod tests;` — causing double-nesting where `use super::*` resolves to the empty `tests` module instead of the parent. Fix by removing the outer `mod tests { ... }` wrapper from the file-based test modules so the content lives at file scope with `use super::*;` directly.
t54.acceptance_criteria: cargo test --workspace compiles and all tests pass
t54.verification_command: cd /home/cole/RustProjects/active/jfc && cargo test --workspace 2>&1 | grep -E "^test result:|^error" | head -20

t72.subject: Replace ext/ext2 slop with macro-driven slash-command registry
t72.description: Collapse input/slash_commands{,_ext,_ext2}.rs (3 files, 88 commands, ~2500 lines) into a single macro_rules! registry that is the SOLE source of truth for dispatch + autocomplete metadata. Grounded in the rust-lang Zulip wg-macros thread "Please help me figure out these very old macro guidelines": functions are preferred for handler LOGIC; a macro is the blessed tool when it "generates a static table and a function guaranteed to take action on all items in that list, significantly reducing human error" (kpreid). Macro defined in the SAME module as its use site. Eliminates the drifted second table in render/overlays.rs.
t72.acceptance_criteria: cargo build -p jfc-ui succeeds; cargo test -p jfc-ui passes; ripgrep finds no slash_commands_ext references; render/overlays.rs no longer defines its own SLASH_COMMANDS const

t74.subject: Extract handler bodies from slash_commands.rs into async fns

t73.subject: Design the slash_commands! macro + SlashCommand descriptor
t73.description: In a new crates/jfc-ui/src/input/slash_commands/registry.rs (or top of slash_commands.rs), define: (1) a `struct SlashSpec { name: &str, aliases: &[&str], help: &str }` descriptor; (2) a `macro_rules! slash_commands!` that takes rows like `"/clear" [] "clear the conversation" => clear_handler` and emits BOTH (a) a `pub(crate) const SLASH_COMMANDS: &[SlashSpec]` static table for autocomplete/help, AND (b) the `match parts[0] { ... }` dispatch arms calling each async handler. Macro must live in the same module as use site per kpreid's guidance. Handler signature: async fn(app: &mut App, parts: &[&str], text: &str, tx: Option<&mpsc::Sender<AppEvent>>). Include a trailing fallthrough hook for the skill-name fallback. Do NOT yet move handler bodies — just establish the macro + descriptor shape and get it compiling with a couple of sample commands.
t73.acceptance_criteria: cargo build -p jfc-ui compiles with the macro expanding 2-3 sample commands; SLASH_COMMANDS const is generated by the macro, not hand-written
